note: Hand-maintained historical consensus vs actual for CPI + NFP + FOMC only. NO scraping, NO AlphaVantage. YOU (the user) must populate this file with real release data — the builder (build_event_scenarios.py) reads it to compute surprise-conditioned magnitude curves. Provenance flips from 'rubric' to 'calibrated' only after a cell reaches n>=30 records. See ARCHITECTURE §4.6 + §8.

_schema.NFP_row: { event:'NFP',  date:'YYYY-MM-DD', consensus:<float k>,    actual:<float k>,    unit:'k'    }
notes: date = the release date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC). CPI typically 2nd Wed of month at 12:30 UTC; NFP first Fri at 12:30 UTC; FOMC varies.
consensus = market median forecast from Bloomberg/Reuters/ForexFactory BEFORE the release.
actual = the officially released figure. For FOMC: stance = post-meeting statement tone.
is_hot: CPI/NFP → actual > consensus. FOMC → stance rank(hawkish=2,neutral=1,dovish=0) > consensus_stance rank.
Realized move is looked up from data/realized_moves.json (60-min move) or data/xau_daily.json (daily proxy).
Add real verified rows below — do NOT guess values. A wrong row is worse than no row (it silently biases the curve).

records._note: EXAMPLE ROW — verify this against the actual June 2026 CPI release before relying on it.
EXAMPLE ROW — verify this against the actual June 2026 NFP release before relying on it.
EXAMPLE ROW — verify this against the actual June 2026 FOMC statement before relying on it.
